title: Imitation and the diffusion of innovation
summary: by Debi Prasad Mohapatra, Vatsala Shreeti Why would a market leader choose not to patent an innovation? We study Samsung's decision to forgo patent protection for dual SIM technology in the Indian mobile handset market.

title: The geography of AI firms
summary: by Kumar Rishabh, Vatsala Shreeti In this paper, we trace the geography and economic characteristics of firms that produce artificial intelligence (AI) products and services. Many economies around the world are evaluating their strategic priorities in AI, yet relatively little is documented about the global distribution of AI production.

title: Quantum Bayesian inference: an exploration
summary: by Jon Frost, Carlos Madeira, Yash Rastogi, Harald Uhlig This paper introduces a framework for performing Bayesian inference using quantum computation. It presents a proof-of-concept quantum algorithm that performs posterior sampling. We provide an accessible introduction to quantum computation for economists and a practical demonstration of quantum-based posterior sampling for Bayesian estimation

title: Labour market flows, unemployment and the Phillips curve
summary: by Enisse Kharroubi, Marius Koechlin We present empirical evidence from the United States demonstrating that labour market flows provide valuable insights into subsequent wage and price inflation.

title: Stablecoin flows and spillovers to FX markets
summary: by Iñaki Aldasoro, Paula Beltrán, Federico Grinberg Using data on four USD-pegged stablecoins and 27 fiat currencies, this paper documents spillovers from stablecoin-based foreign exchange (FX) to traditional FXmarkets. title: Financial stability limits on fiscal space
summary: by Fabrizio Zampolli Conventional indicators of fiscal sustainability, such as the interest rate-growth differential that focus on long-term drivers do not always incorporate fluctuating financial conditions and risk. title: Global banking and geopolitics through time
summary: by Lorenz Emter, Laura Kuitunen, Arnaud Mehl, Peter McQuade, Swapan-Kumar Pradhan, Goetz von Peter This paper examines asymmetries in the effects of geopolitical events on international bank credit, contrasting adverse events, such as the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, with positive events like the fall of the Berlin Wall in 1989.

title: Introducing BISTRO: a foundational model for unconditional and conditional forecasting of macroeconomic time series
summary: by Batuhan Koyuncu, Byeungchun Kwon, Marco Jacopo Lombardi, Fernando Perez-Cruz, Hyun Song Shin This article introduces the BIS Time-series Regression Oracle (BISTRO), a general purpose time series model for macroeconomic forecasting. title: A public-private partnership? Central bank funding and credit supply
summary: by Matthieu Chavaz, David Elliott, Win Monroe We exploit the surprise announcement and subsequent amendment of a central bank funding scheme to test how public liquidity provision affects credit market outcomes. Contrary to the notion that public liquidity is primarily a substitute for private liquidity, banks that are more exposed to stress in private wholesale funding markets use less central ba

title: Tokenomics and blockchain fragmentation
summary: by Hyun Song Shin Money is a coordination device underpinned by strong network effects: the more others accept a form of money, the more I wish to adopt it too. The decentralisation agenda of public permissionless blockchains undercuts these network effects and leads to fragmentation of the monetary landscape.

title: Robots, ICT and employment: evidence from advanced and emerging EU countries
summary: by Costanza Bosone, Leonardo Gambacorta, Paolo Giudici, Enisse Kharroubi, Ulf Lewrick We study how robot adoption and investment in information and communication technologies (ICT) jointly shape sectoral employment across 20 European Union (EU) countries over the period 1995-2020. title: Generative AI for surveys on payment apps: AI views on privacy and technology
summary: by Koji Takahashi, Joon Suk Park This study uses ChatGPT to simulate survey responses about payment apps, focusing on privacy and perceived benefits. By designing prompts that mirror real user characteristics, the generated responses align with findings from a Dutch survey, especially when grouped by privacy concern. Privacy-concerned agents view apps less favorably, while users show more positive

title: Dollar funding and housing markets: the role of non-US global banks
summary: by Torsten Ehlers, Mathias Hoffmann, Alexander Raabe House prices co-move considerably across countries. We show how non-US global banks and their exposure to US dollar funding conditions help explain this comovement. ..

title: Lending to vulnerable households and consumption: evidence from Korea
summary: by Jieun Lee, Ilhyock Shim Using a large quarterly consumer credit panel dataset from Korea covering 2017–2023, we present four key findings on the characteristics, lending sources, and macroeconomic consequences of vulnerable borrowers-namely, delinquent borrowers whose debt repayment is overdue at least 30 days, and borrowers whose debt service ratio is higher than 50% but who are not delinquent

title: Passive investors and loan spreads
summary: by Konrad Adler, Sebastian Doerr, Sonya Zhu

title: Adoption and welfare effects of payment innovations: the case of digital wallets in Peru
summary: by Arturo Andia, José Aurazo, Marcelo Paliza Digital wallets like Yape and Plin have gained widespread popularity in Peru, allowing users to make instant payments through quick response (QR) codes or mobile phone numbers. title: The perils of narrowing fiscal spaces
summary: by Hanno Kase, Leonardo Melosi, Sebastian Rast, Matthias Rottner When public debt is elevated, the fiscal cost of fighting inflation rises sharply, as interest rate hikes increase government interest expenditures. title: A tractable menu cost model with an aggregate markup drift
summary: by Ko Munakata This paper extends the menu cost model of Gertler and Leahy (2008) by introducing a drift in the aggregate markup. Assuming that the drift is always negative and not large, consistent with moderate and positive trend inflation, the paper analytically characterizes firms' value function and markup distribution. title: Monetary policy and private equity acquisitions: tracing the links
summary: by Fernando Avalos, Boris Hofmann, José María Serena Garralda Private equity funds play an increasingly important role in financial systems. Yet, the impact of monetary policy on their activity has been little explored so far. title: AI adoption, productivity and employment: evidence from European firms
summary: by Iñaki Aldasoro, Leonardo Gambacorta, Rozalia Pal, Debora Revoltella, Christoph Weiss, Marcin Wolski This paper provides new evidence on how the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) affects productivity and employment in Europe...

title: The macro-financial impact of economic policy uncertainty in Latin America
summary: by Ana Aguilar, Rafael Guerra, Carola Müller, Alexandre Tombini This paper investigates the impact of domestic economic policy uncertainty (EPU) on macroeconomic and financial variables in emerging market economies, focusing on Latin America. title: Embracing gen AI: a comparison of Italian and US households
summary: by David Loschiavo, Olivier Armantier, Antonio Dalla Zuanna, Leonardo Gambacorta, Mirko Moscatelli, Ilaria Supino This paper explores the household adoption of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in the United States and Italy, leveraging survey data to compare usage patterns, demographic influences, and employment sectoral composition effects. title: Artificial intelligence and growth in advanced and emerging economies: short-run impact
summary: by Leonardo Gambacorta, Enisse Kharroubi, Aaron Mehrotra, Tommaso Oliviero This paper investigates whether the positive effects of generative artificial intelligence (gen AI) on growth rate of value added differ across countries in the short run.

title: Inflation and the joint bond-FX spanning puzzle
summary: by Andreas Schrimpf, Markus Sihvonen We investigate the relationship between inflation and excess returns in bond and foreign exchange (FX) markets, specifically addressing the "spanning puzzle".
